mcu: NXP Cortex-M4 (LPC546xx-class) — M_CAN/FDCAN + ADC + SCT/CTimer; FreeRTOS. (peripheral bases tentative)
generated_from: motor-sensor-decode workflow (bootstrap from vector table: 0 -> 210 fns; then 8 Sonnet map agents carve+classify -> 218 fns)
note: Motor position/current/temperature sensor ECU. Reads a magnetic angle/position sensor (@0xf400, 12-byte block) + ADC, reports over CAN-FD (@0x40006000); has a flash-update path, a watchdog (WWDT 0xAA/0x55), and a BLE-GATT/key-sensor link (VMKYS task). main=FUN_0000375c (peripheral bring-up + xTaskCreate@0x29ec + scheduler). 5 VanMoof tasks: motor_sensor_task, CanTX, can, VMKYS, vm. Inline jump-table/data blocks of FUN_375c (0x3b10/0x3d2e/0x4028/0x4326/0x4710/0x4bc6) are excluded (not functions).

status_legend.vanmoof: VanMoof app code (sensor sampling + CAN reporting + tasks + glue)

functions.name: vector_table
functions.note: Cortex-M vector table: SP_init=0x20008000 at word[0], Reset=0xDC1(thumb) at word[1], then NMI/HardFault/MemManage/BusFault/UsageFault/SVCall=vPortSVCHandler(0xF61)/PendSV=xPortPendSVHandler(0xF01)/SysTick=xPortSysTickHandler(0x5B81), then 60 external IRQ entries 0x40-0x133. Data region, not executab

functions.name: vmfw_header
functions.note: VanMoof firmware image header: magic VMFW, version 0x01056000 (v1.5.0-main), CRC 0xEDE5DA94, length 0x6204, build timestamp Jan 29 2024 14:50:32, SRAM base 0x20000000. Data, not code.

functions.name: crt0_startup_body
functions.note: C runtime startup inline code (part of startup flow): iterates copy table to initialize .data from flash, zeros .BSS segments word-by-word, then calls main initializer at 0x375c. Followed by PSP setup code at 0x290 that sets CONTROL.SPSEL=2, loads PSP, clears BASEPRI, and branches to the first task

functions.name: vPortStartFirstTask
functions.note: FreeRTOS Cortex-M port: loads initial MSP from current TCB's stack top pointer, enables IRQ and FIQ (CPSIE i/f), DSB+ISB, then issues SVC #2 to transfer control to the first FreeRTOS task via vPortSVCHandler.

functions.name: xPortPendSVHandler_body
functions.note: FreeRTOS PendSV context-switch body (inline in gap): saves callee-saved regs (r2-r11) + LR onto PSP using stmdb, stores PSP to current TCB, raises BASEPRI to mask IRQs, calls vTaskSwitchContext (0x36F0), clears BASEPRI, restores saved registers from new TCB's PSP, returns via bx r3.

functions.name: vPortSVCHandler_dispatch
functions.note: FreeRTOS SVC handler dispatch stub (inline in gap): tests LR bit[2] to choose MSP vs PSP, loads vector from pointer table, bx to handler.

functions.name: fault_handler_spin
functions.note: Fault spin handler: performs DSB, writes priority group bits into NVIC AIRCR (or similar register at 0xE000ED0C region via constant tables at 0x3A0/0x3A4), DSB again, then loops forever. Called from the error/fault path.

functions.name: NVIC_EnableIRQ
functions.note: CMSIS NVIC_EnableIRQ(IRQn): computes ISER register index (IRQn>>5) and bit mask (1<<(IRQn&31)), writes to NVIC ISER base at 0xE000E100. Returns without action for negative (reserved) IRQ numbers.

functions.name: unused_irq_stub_table
functions.note: Table of 30+ unused/reserved IRQ handler stubs (0x3C4-0x49F): each is a pair [b . / nop] (infinite spin). Two real stubs embedded at 0x438 and 0x450 each do: ldr r2,[ptr_to_state]; ldr r1,[r2]; ldr r3,[r3_ptr]; ldr r0,[extra]; bx r3 — likely reset/reinit trampolines. No separate functions carved due

functions.name: peripheral_is_enabled
functions.note: Reads system status register 0x40000A18 bit[6]; if set, loads and returns a non-zero constant; otherwise returns 0. Likely queries whether a specific peripheral (ADC or SPI) clock/enable is active.

functions.name: can_get_status_flags
functions.note: Reads M_CAN PSR register (base+0xB8) and ECR (base+0x98) to determine bus state. Returns 0x8000 if error-passive (PSR.EP set) or bus-off (PSR.BO set). Returns 0 if nominal. App-level CAN health check.

functions.name: gpio_irq_dispatch
functions.note: GPIO interrupt dispatcher: given a port index and status word, routes to registered callbacks. Checks a mode byte per port: if 0, calls single callback[0](status); otherwise iterates bits 0-6, calling each registered bit-callback[bit](status) that is non-null.

functions.name: gpio_ch0_irq_handler
functions.note: GPIO controller 1, channel 0 ISR: reads peripheral status from 0x40004000, invokes registered callback(0, status) if non-null, calls FUN_00004dd0 to clear interrupt if bit[0] not set.

functions.name: gpio_ch1_irq_handler
functions.note: GPIO controller 1, channel 1 ISR. Same pattern as ch0 but index=1, checks bit[2] of status for clear condition.

functions.name: gpio_ch2_irq_handler
functions.note: GPIO controller 1, channel 2 ISR. Index=2, checks bit[3] of status for clear condition.

functions.name: gpio_ch3_irq_handler
functions.note: GPIO controller 1, channel 3 ISR. Index=3, checks bit[4].

functions.name: gpio_ch4_irq_handler
functions.note: GPIO controller 1, channel 4 ISR. Index=4, checks bit[5].

functions.name: gpio_ch5_irq_handler
functions.note: GPIO controller 1, channel 5 ISR. Index=5, checks bit[6].

functions.name: gpio_ch6_irq_handler
functions.note: GPIO controller 1, channel 6 ISR. Index=6, checks bit[7].

functions.name: gpio_ch7_irq_handler
functions.note: GPIO controller 1, channel 7 ISR. Index=7, checks bit[8].

functions.name: gpio2_ch0_irq_handler
functions.note: GPIO controller 2, channel 0 ISR: reads status from second GPIO peripheral at DAT_0000071C, invokes callback(0, status), clears if bit[0] not set. Separate peripheral base from the ch0-ch7 above.

functions.name: gpio2_ch1_irq_handler
functions.note: GPIO controller 2, channel 1 ISR. Index=1 on second GPIO controller, bit[1] clear test.

functions.name: can_is_fd_mode
functions.note: Returns true (non-zero) if CAN controller is configured in FD mode: reads byte at CAN config base + 6, compares to 3.

functions.name: can_tx_frame_abort
functions.note: Aborts a pending CAN TX frame: writes 0x60 to TX buffer abort field (+0x28), calls vtable entry[1] to trigger hardware abort, conditionally adjusts TX size field if current value is 0xA0000 by subtracting a scaled term.

functions.name: can_tx_dispatch
functions.note: CAN TX dispatcher: checks FD mode and NVIC status register (0x40000FFC bits[3:0]); selects one of three vtable function pointers to call with the TX frame argument.

functions.name: can_tx_submit
functions.note: Submits a CAN frame to the hardware TX buffer: selects vtable handler at +0x2C (classic) or +0x38 (FD) depending on can_is_fd_mode, then dispatches the frame.

functions.name: timeout_table_register
functions.note: Registers a (deadline, callback) pair in a fixed 8-entry timeout table (indexed 0-7). Scans for first slot where key==0 and stores the pair. Used by the timer subsystem to track per-channel timeouts.

functions.name: tick_counter_increment
functions.note: Increments the global system tick counter (*DAT_0000082C += 1). Called from the SysTick/tick hook path.

functions.name: tick_deadline_update
functions.note: Updates the global tick deadline: reads the head of the timer queue through a linked-list pointer; if the queue is empty sets deadline to 0xFFFFFFFF, otherwise reads the deadline from the head node (at node+0xC dereferenced).

functions.name: queue_state_check
functions.note: Returns CAN queue state: 1 if TX queue is empty (*DAT_0000086C==0), 2 if TX queue non-empty but RX queue empty (*DAT_00000870==0), 0 if both queues have content.

functions.name: timer_event_schedule
functions.note: Schedules a periodic timer event: stores deadline (param_2) and links self-pointer into param_1. Handles tick wraparound for both forward and backward deadline ordering. Calls FUN_00004e60 to insert into the sorted timer list.

functions.name: heap_free_block
functions.note: FreeRTOS heap_4.c vPortFree equivalent: walks a singly-linked free list in ascending address order, inserts the freed block at the correct position, and coalesces with adjacent free blocks (both predecessor and successor).

functions.name: assert_failed_spin
functions.note: Assertion failure trap: checks a sentinel value (*DAT_0000091C), calls FUN_00004EA0 (error/disable function), then loops forever. Equivalent to configASSERT() fail path.

functions.name: critical_section_exit
functions.note: FreeRTOS portEXIT_CRITICAL_FROM_ISR equivalent: calls FUN_00004EA0 (re-enable IRQs at saved mask), increments nesting counter, executes DSB+ISB.

functions.name: critical_section_leave
functions.note: FreeRTOS taskEXIT_CRITICAL_FROM_ISR: asserts nesting count > 0 (calls assert_failed if 0), decrements nesting counter, if counter reaches zero calls FUN_00004ED4 to restore interrupt mask.

functions.name: tick_delay_compute
functions.note: Computes a tick-based delay: given a (start_tick, delay_ticks) pair, adjusts the expiry relative to the current tick counter, handling the case where the timer list head matches. Calls critical_section_leave on exit.

functions.name: spi_write_bytes
functions.note: Writes a byte buffer to SPI FIFO at peripheral_base+8. Handles byte-alignment: writes trailing bytes one-by-one until 4-byte aligned, then writes word-at-a-time for bulk, then flushes remaining bytes. Peripheral base loaded from DAT_00000A00.

functions.name: can_rx_enqueue
functions.note: Enqueues a received CAN frame into the RX buffer: selects the write function from a vtable at offset +0x24 (classic CAN) or +0x30 (FD mode), passes the frame and a zero padding arg.

functions.name: can_rx_dequeue
functions.note: Dequeues a CAN frame from the RX buffer: selects read function at vtable +0xC (FD) or uses DAT_00000A54 (classic), passes 0x200 as max frame size.

functions.name: gpio_set_pin
functions.note: Sets or clears a GPIO pin by index: looks up (port, pin) from a 2-byte lookup table at 0xAB4; writes IOCON clock-enable via 0x40000000 region; then based on direction (param_3[0]) and mode (param_3[1]) writes to GPIO SET/CLR/DIR registers at 0x4008E000.

functions.name: can_tx_peek
functions.note: Non-destructive peek at the CAN TX buffer head: selects vtable read function at +0x40 (classic) or +0x4C (FD), passes frame pointer and 0x200 size. Does not advance read pointer.

functions.name: can_tx_poke
functions.note: Advances the CAN TX buffer read pointer (consumes the peeked frame): selects vtable function at +0x8 (FD) or DAT_00000B1C (classic), passes 0x200 and an extra DAT arg.

functions.name: can_bus_error_recover
functions.note: M_CAN bus-off recovery sequence: writes CCCR=2 (init mode) at base+0x84, DSB+ISB, calls FUN_00004DD0 with command 5 (bus-off recovery), then writes CCCR=2 at base+0x184 to release. Restores CAN to operational mode after bus-off.

functions.name: freertos_tick_hook
functions.note: FreeRTOS vApplicationTickHook: if not in nested ISR, increments tick counter via tick_counter_increment, checks for expired timer deadlines (calls FUN_00004F58 if expired), updates next deadline, returns 1 if pending work (DAT_00000C34 != 0). If nested ISR, increments a nested-tick counter and retur

functions.name: (literal pool)
functions.note: 8-byte literal pool data consumed by FUN_0000b50 via ldr.w from [0x0c40] and [0x0c44]; not a function

functions.name: freertos_timer_decrement_and_dispatch
functions.note: FreeRTOS prvProcessExpiredTimer equivalent — decrements timer tick count, dispatches expired software timer callbacks via FUN_00000b50, calls FUN_00004ee2 (xTaskResumeAll) if work was done

functions.name: freertos_queue_free_block
functions.note: FreeRTOS prvReturnItemToFreeList equivalent — validates block magic, returns memory block back to free list, increments free count

functions.name: freertos_queue_dequeue_and_resume
functions.note: FreeRTOS queue helper — spins calling a function pointer (param_1[1]) on param_1[2] with timeout 0x7fffffff until it returns 0, then resumes via FUN_00004f58

functions.name: NMI_Handler
functions.note: Cortex-M NMI exception handler — saves FPU context and calls fault reporter FUN_00004f58

functions.name: HardFault_Handler
functions.note: Cortex-M HardFault handler — infinite loop trap

functions.name: MemManage_Handler
functions.note: Cortex-M MemManage fault handler — infinite loop trap

functions.name: BusFault_Handler
functions.note: Cortex-M BusFault handler — infinite loop trap

functions.name: UsageFault_Handler
functions.note: Cortex-M UsageFault handler — infinite loop trap

functions.name: FUN_00000db8
functions.note: Reserved exception slot handler — likely NOP/infinite-loop stub for unused exception vector at 0x1C

functions.name: DebugMon_Handler
functions.note: Cortex-M DebugMon exception handler — infinite loop trap or NOP

functions.name: Reset_Handler
functions.note: CMSIS startup Reset_Handler — copies .data section via freertos_queue_free_block, zeroes .bss, then calls SystemInit and application main. Shared with imx8_bridge sibling (same 0xdc0 reset).

functions.name: freertos_queue_recv_item
functions.note: FreeRTOS internal queue receive helper — validates queue list head pointer, calls vTaskSwitchContext (FUN_00004f58) on the item

functions.name: freertos_queue_send_from_isr
functions.note: FreeRTOS xQueueSendFromISR inner loop — iterates param_1+0x45 count, calls freertos_queue_recv_item, signals waiting task via DAT_00000f14, resets count byte to 0xFF

functions.name: xPortPendSVHandler
functions.note: FreeRTOS Cortex-M4 PendSV context-switch handler — saves/restores task registers including FPU, switches pxCurrentTCB

functions.name: freertos_queue_signal_waiting_task
functions.note: FreeRTOS queue signal helper — checks scheduler running (DAT_00000fb4), ORs 0x80000000 into param_1[0] (task notification), validates param_1[3], calls vTaskSwitchContext

functions.name: vPortSVCHandler
functions.note: FreeRTOS Cortex-M4 SVC handler — first-task launch, sets PSP and drops to task mode

functions.name: freertos_queue_pend_wait_loop
functions.note: FreeRTOS queue wait-for-space inner loop — spins while queue full flag is set, yields via FUN_00004ee2 (xTaskResumeAll) if multiple tasks waiting, then calls taskENTER_CRITICAL and vTaskSwitchContext

functions.name: FUN_00000fcc
functions.note: FreeRTOS queue wait continuation fragment at +4 offset into freertos_queue_pend_wait_loop body — entry point from different branch of queue send

functions.name: FUN_00000fd0
functions.note: FreeRTOS queue wait fragment — inner wait loop body shared by multiple queue paths via -Os ICF

functions.name: FUN_00000fd4
functions.note: FreeRTOS queue wait fragment variant — enters loop after scheduler-suspend check

functions.name: FUN_00000fd8
functions.note: FreeRTOS queue wait fragment variant

functions.name: FUN_00000fdc
functions.note: FreeRTOS queue wait fragment variant — enters directly into FUN_00004ee2 call

functions.name: FUN_00000fe0
functions.note: FreeRTOS queue wait fragment variant

functions.name: FUN_00000fe8
functions.note: FreeRTOS vTaskSwitchContext tail — loads TCB list head and jumps to FUN_00004f58

functions.name: FUN_00000fec
functions.note: FreeRTOS vTaskSwitchContext direct tail-call — no-arg tail into FUN_00004f58

functions.name: FUN_00000ff0
functions.note: FreeRTOS queue count decrement pair — decrements two counters (uxMessagesWaiting and uxSemaphoreCount), calls taskEXIT_CRITICAL

functions.name: FUN_00000ff4
functions.note: FreeRTOS queue store-and-decrement — stores r3 to *r6, decrements *r4, calls taskEXIT_CRITICAL

functions.name: FUN_00000ff8
functions.note: FreeRTOS queue counter decrement — decrements *r4 by 1, calls taskEXIT_CRITICAL

functions.name: FUN_00000ffc
functions.note: FreeRTOS taskEXIT_CRITICAL tail — direct tail-call to FUN_0000093c

functions.name: FUN_00001000
functions.note: FreeRTOS queue/data-section copy loop body — copies .data init values via freertos_queue_free_block, then branches back into wait loop

functions.name: FUN_00001008
functions.note: FreeRTOS .data copy loop tail — calls freertos_queue_free_block then branches into wait loop

functions.name: thunk_FUN_00000fd0
functions.note: Thunk jumping to FUN_00000fd0 queue wait loop

functions.name: FUN_00001020
functions.note: FreeRTOS xQueueSend inner path — loads queue handle from DAT_0000107c, calls FUN_00004f58 (vTaskSwitchContext) with queue+4

functions.name: FUN_00001024
functions.note: FreeRTOS xQueueSend inner path variant — same as 0x1020 but different entry

functions.name: FUN_00001028
functions.note: FreeRTOS queue send tail — calls FUN_00004f58(*r6+4)

functions.name: FUN_0000102c
functions.note: FreeRTOS queue send tail — calls FUN_00004f58(param_1+4)

functions.name: FUN_00001030
functions.note: FreeRTOS vTaskSwitchContext tail call with no arg

functions.name: FUN_00001034
functions.note: FreeRTOS timer list insert — checks for 0xFFFFFFFF (max ticks), computes new expiry tick, writes to timer list bucket, updates uxTaskNumber min-ticks field, calls xQueueSend (0x4e48/0x4e60)

functions.name: FUN_00001038
functions.note: FreeRTOS timer list insert variant (without 0xFFFFFFFF special case)

functions.name: FUN_00001050
functions.note: FreeRTOS timer list insert tail — calls FUN_00004e60 to insert into overflow list

functions.name: FUN_00001068
functions.note: FreeRTOS timer min-tick update — updates *DAT_0000108c if r4 < current min

functions.name: FUN_0000106c
functions.note: FreeRTOS timer min-tick update tail (no preceding call)

functions.name: FUN_00001070
functions.note: FreeRTOS conditional min-tick update — if(r4<param_3) *param_4=r4

functions.name: FUN_00001074
functions.note: FreeRTOS conditional store — if(CY && !ZR) *in_r3=r4 (carry-without-zero = unsigned greater)

functions.name: FUN_00001090
functions.note: FreeRTOS timer list re-insert with task wakeup — inserts into timer list at +0x18 offset, then calls FUN_00001020 (xQueueSend) to signal timer service task

functions.name: FUN_00001094
functions.note: FreeRTOS timer list insert tail with wakeup (variant)

functions.name: FUN_00001098
functions.note: FreeRTOS timer list insert with explicit offset and wakeup

functions.name: freertos_timer_tick_process
functions.note: FreeRTOS prvTimerTask tick processing — validates timer handle, checks interrupt context, decrements reload count, updates TCB linked list pointer, signals waiting task if queue ready

functions.name: thunk_FUN_0000093c
functions.note: Thunk to FUN_0000093c (taskEXIT_CRITICAL / xTaskResumeAll)

functions.name: freertos_timer_queue_send_isr
functions.note: FreeRTOS xQueueSendToBackFromISR for timer queue — conditional path: if ZR set calls freertos_queue_send_from_isr chain, else checks timer priority (0x115c logic), signals queue item arrival via FUN_00001090

functions.name: freertos_timer_priority_check
functions.note: FreeRTOS timer priority comparison — compares timer->uxTimerPriority with current task priority, updates timer->uxBasePriority and list position if needed

functions.name: FUN_00001164
functions.note: FreeRTOS timer priority check variant with explicit param3/param4

functions.name: FUN_0000116c
functions.note: FreeRTOS timer priority update with list-position check — updates +0x18 field, conditional FUN_00004f58 if list position matches

functions.name: FUN_00001174
functions.note: FreeRTOS timer priority update tail — unconditional write to +0x18, conditional FUN_00004f58

functions.name: FUN_000011a4
functions.note: FreeRTOS conditional store + list insert — if(carry and not zero) *param_3=param_4, then FUN_00004e48 (list remove), then taskEXIT_CRITICAL

functions.name: freertos_queue_receive_blocking
functions.note: FreeRTOS xQueueReceive blocking path — calls freertos_queue_send_from_isr, freertos_timer_decrement_and_dispatch, taskENTER_CRITICAL, decrements queue message count, updates TCB list pointers, calls xTaskResumeAll if task woke

functions.name: FUN_00001204
functions.note: FreeRTOS queue notify tail — if in_r3 != 0 and freertos_queue_recv_item returns nonzero, calls xTaskResumeAll, then taskEXIT_CRITICAL

functions.name: FUN_00001234
functions.note: FreeRTOS taskEXIT_CRITICAL tail (bare)

functions.name: freertos_task_priority_set
functions.note: FreeRTOS vTaskPrioritySet inner logic — finds TCB via param_1+8, computes new effective priority (max of base and inherited), validates ready-list membership, updates uxPriority and uxBasePriority, moves task in ready list if priority changed

functions.name: freertos_assert_failed
functions.note: FreeRTOS configASSERT failure handler — calls FUN_00004ea0 (enter critical / disable IRQ) then infinite loops

functions.name: FUN_000012c4
functions.note: FreeRTOS sorted-list conditional insert — if(carry && !zero) *param_3=param_4, then FUN_00004e48(param_4*0x14+r5, param_1) (list insert at computed offset), taskEXIT_CRITICAL

functions.name: can_tx_state_set_active
functions.note: App layer: sets CAN TX object state to ACTIVE. Acquires critical section (FUN_00000920), checks byte at *piVar1+0x68 != 0x02 (not already transmitting), copies *(piVar1+0x64) and sets byte 0x68=1, signals CanTX task via FUN_00001020(param_1) if param_1 != 0. Called after a CAN frame descriptor is pl

functions.name: freertos_timer_stop_and_remove
functions.note: FreeRTOS xTimerStop / vTimerStop inner logic — if timer is in active list (param_1[0x58]==DAT_000013ec), removes its callback from callback-table array (8-entry scan), removes timer block via FUN_00000d0c, drains waiting-task list via FUN_00000f5c, optionally triggers SysTick reset (writes 0x80 to 0

functions.name: freertos_queue_send_blocking
functions.note: FreeRTOS xQueueSend (blocking) inner path — validates queue ptr, checks scheduler state, enters critical, decrements available count (param_1+0x38), calls FUN_00005128 (copy-to-queue), wakes waiting task via freertos_queue_recv_item, calls xTaskResumeAll

functions.name: can_event_fsm_dispatch
functions.note: App layer CAN interrupt event FSM dispatcher. Reads M_CAN IR (Interrupt Register) from param_1+0x50, clears the handled bit in param_1+0x54 (ILS/ILE), decodes event type: TX complete (bit0=0x1 or bit4=0x10 -> code 0x183d/0x1842), RX FIFO new message (bit9=0x200 -> code 0x1839, clears RXFIFO0 pending

functions.name: can_controller_init
functions.note: CAN controller hardware initializer. Enables peripheral clocks (0x40000148=0x8000000, 0x40000228=0x8000000), configures GPIO pins for CAN TX/RX (0x40000300/320/32c/38c/394/30c), selects NBTP bit-timing from calibration table based on nominal bitrate: 0x7c1e (low speed), 0x6c16 (mid speed), 0x5811 (h

functions.name: can_tx_task
functions.note: App layer CAN TX FreeRTOS task (CanTX task, string at 0x616C). Infinite loop: (1) blocks on freertos_queue_send_blocking(param_1[0], &local_buf, 500ms) waiting for a frame descriptor; (2) builds 29-bit extended CAN ID header: local_38 = (id & 0x1FFFFFFF) | 0x40000000 (XTD bit set); (3) if TX buffer

functions.name: queue_stream_advance_read_ptr
functions.note: Advances circular buffer read pointer; decrements ref-count and signals task completion (via FUN_00004f58) when buffer fully drained. Body 0x1984–0x1a17; literal pool 0x1a18–0x1a29.

functions.name: can_tx_completion_state_machine
functions.note: CAN TX event callback; state codes 0x1839/0x1848/0x1846 drive ACK/NACK/retry paths; builds CAN TX buffer header via FUN_0000530a, triggers PendSV (E000ED04=0x10000000) for deferred reschedule.

functions.name: queue_send_item
functions.note: Post item to FreeRTOS queue: validates handle, calls queue_stream_advance_read_ptr, signals waiting tasks via FUN_0000093c. Task-context version; checks scheduler running state.

functions.name: queue_send_item_irq_variant
functions.note: ISR-variant of queue_send_item with unaff_r4 Ghidra artifact; functionally identical body 0x1b18–0x1c01. No callers found — likely dead code or ICF-duplicate.

functions.name: queue_send_dispatch
functions.note: Routes queue send: if queue-type==6 calls FUN_000053d8 (timer daemon path), else calls queue_send_item with ISR-safe flag derived from FUN_00000854 scheduler state.

functions.name: can_channel_abort_and_disconnect
functions.note: Marks channel inactive (byte+9=0); if queue handle non-null and item count nonzero sends disconnect message (code 5) via queue_send_dispatch; resets RCC clock gate at 0x4000038c=0x40000000.

functions.name: queue_node_unlink
functions.note: Pops head node from singly-linked list: if head->next==0 calls FUN_00004f58 (task unblock); else swaps list head pointer to next node. Used by can_tx_manager_loop to dequeue pending TX items.

functions.name: can_tx_manager_loop
functions.note: Main CAN TX manager event loop (body 0x1d5c–0x1f42): blocks on queue via FUN_000013f8; switch(msg_type): 0/1/2/6/7=open+send new frame, 3/8=clear active flag, 4/9=set TX data pointer, 5=abort if not active; periodically updates SysTick watermark and calls queue_node_unlink.

functions.name: can_tx_buffer_enable
functions.note: Sets FDCAN TX buffer pending/enable bitmasks: ORs param_1/param_2 into regs +0x10/+0x14; if persistent_flag also ORs into +0x18/+0x1c; delays 100 ticks via FUN_00005154; then calls FUN_000054a8 to arm TX.

functions.name: can_connection_handoff
functions.note: Transitions connection state +0x68 from 1→2; if DAT_00002018 (peer queue) valid, copies TX buffer header via FUN_00004e48 and sets high-water flag if new sequence count exceeds previous.

functions.name: can_connection_abort
functions.note: Task-context connection close: disables scheduler, sets state +0x68=2; if state was 1 (active), unblocks pending task via FUN_00004f58; then yields via FUN_0000093c.

functions.name: can_channel_open
functions.note: Opens CAN TX channel: validates handle not null, state not already 2; sets state=2, calls optional pre-open callback (param_1[7]), then configures FDCAN TX via FUN_000054e0. Returns -2/−3 on error, 0 on success.

functions.name: can_channel_open_from_irq
functions.note: Thin wrapper: calls FUN_00000b28 (basepri mask / enter critical) then can_channel_open. For use from ISR context.

functions.name: can_tx_irq_enable_and_open
functions.note: Enables CAN TX-done interrupt bits (+4 |= 2 then &= ~2 toggle), then calls can_channel_open; on failure and pending list non-null posts error via FUN_000053d8 and triggers PendSV (0xe000ed04=0x10000000).

functions.name: can_tx_send_with_flow_control
functions.note: CAN TX send with watermark: checks buffer occupancy vs threshold (0x8c); encodes frame via FUN_0000520a; if overflow clears *param_1 and retries, updates connection state via can_connection_abort on full overflow; handles task and IRQ contexts.

functions.name: can_tx_abort_reset
functions.note: Checks global lock (ptr at 0x2474); if already set asserts via FUN_00004ea0; otherwise acquires mutex (FUN_00000820 timeout=10) then calls FUN_00001020 (no-return task unblock/abort).

functions.name: peripheral_hw_init
functions.note: Full CAN/ADC/timer/clock hardware init (468 bytes, NEWLY CARVED): enables CAN TX done flag, programs FDCAN nominal+data bit timing (+0x88/+0x8c |=0x100), sets clocks (0x40000220=0x800, 0x40000240=0x800), configures SPI/ADC peripheral at 0x40004000 (prescaler=5), enables NVIC IRQ 33, programs FDCAN T

functions.name: flash_read_sectors
functions.note: Reads flash sectors at base 0x8000+offset in 512-byte blocks via FUN_00000790 (flash read page); advances via FUN_000009bc (flash page advance/erase toggle); accumulates result into *param_4; validates param_2+param_3 <= 0x7400. Returns 0=ok, 1=read_error, 2=bad_params.

functions.name: can_send_status_frame_382
functions.note: Acquires CAN TX slot (FUN_00004d3e, msg-id 0x382, 3-byte), waits semaphore 100 ms, copies 8-byte payload from literal pool at 0x25e8 into CAN buffer, sends (FUN_00005914), releases semaphore (FUN_000054a8).

functions.name: can_send_data_frame_8887
functions.note: Sends 30-byte sensor data frame on CAN (msg-id 0x8887). Copies 7 words + 1 halfword from caller-supplied buffer into CAN TX buffer, then acquire/release cycle.

functions.name: can_tx_queue_flush
functions.note: Waits on CAN TX semaphore (100 ms), drains the TX queue (FUN_000059fa), clears pending TX mask bits in obj+0x10/0x14, zeroes obj+0x18/0x1c, releases semaphore.

functions.name: can_send_status_frame_2a1
functions.note: Sends 1-word status payload on CAN (msg-id 0x2A1). Word sourced from literal pool at 0x2720. Same acquire/wait/release pattern as 0x2580.

functions.name: flash_write_sector
functions.note: Writes a region of flash. param_1=flash-obj, param_2=byte-offset (max 0x7400), param_3=src-ptr, param_4=byte-len. Uses 0x200-byte sector granularity; issues erase+program (cmd 0xF200) via FUN_00000790; verifies with FUN_00000aec.

functions.name: heap_malloc
functions.note: dlmalloc-style heap allocator. First call initialises a 0x5400-byte heap pool from a bss region. Walks free-list, splits blocks, 8-byte-aligns all allocations. Returns aligned ptr or 0 on OOM. Calls FUN_00000c48 (disable-IRQ critical section), FUN_000008b0 (heap coalesce).

functions.name: freertos_task_alloc_init
functions.note: FreeRTOS inner task allocator. Allocs 0x3C bytes for TCB via heap_malloc, selects stack-init function pointer from a table (checked via FUN_00000764/FUN_00000754 for Cortex-M port variant), calls port stack initialiser, then FUN_00004f20 (prvInitialiseNewTask field setup). Returns TCB ptr or 0.

functions.name: gatt_attr_write_notify
functions.note: Reads current attribute value (0x200-byte scratch buffer via FUN_000007d4), optionally sets notification-enable bits (0x70) in attr control byte, writes updated value back via FUN_00000a04. Returns true on error.

functions.name: xTaskCreate
functions.note: FreeRTOS xTaskCreate. Allocs stack (param_3*4 bytes) + TCB (0x6C bytes). Copies task name (≤0x13 chars), initialises TCB fields, writes Cortex-M initial stack frame with register fill patterns (R0–R12=0x00010101..0x12121212, xPSR=0x01000000, LR=0xFFFFFFFD). Links TCB into ready-list at given priorit

functions.name: timer_queue_init
functions.note: FreeRTOS timer-service task/queue one-time init. Calls FUN_00004e32 twice (queue handles), then FUN_00005af6(10, 0x10) to create timer queue (depth=10, item=16 bytes), binds callback via FUN_000007fc. Tail-calls FUN_0000093c (scheduler yield/start).

functions.name: xTimerCreate
functions.note: FreeRTOS xTimerCreate. param_1=handle-out-ptr, param_2=period_ms (0x7FFFFFFF=infinite), param_3=auto-reload, param_4=timer-id, param_5=callback. Allocs 0x28-byte timer struct, converts period, initialises timer_queue_init on first call, starts timer via FUN_00001c20. Returns false (0) on success.

functions.name: watchdog_feed_timer_cb
functions.note: Watchdog feed callback. Creates/rearms a 5000 ms software timer (xTimerCreate). When tick count matches, writes 0xAA then 0x55 to watchdog reload register (DAT_00002d60+8) under primask to service the hardware watchdog.

functions.name: can_controller_init
functions.note: CAN peripheral init. Sets busy flag in CAN obj, clears peripheral enable at 0x4000038c=0, creates 5000 ms watchdog timer, calls FUN_00000b28 (CAN clock+GPIO setup) and FUN_000015fc (M_CAN filter/mode config), programs acceptance filter registers CAN+0x88/+0x8C with mask 0x101 ORed into existing valu

functions.name: can_send_brake_status_4a1
functions.note: Sends brake-engaged bit on CAN (msg-id 0x4A1). Reads bit-0 of param_3[0] as brake flag, stores in DAT_00002e78+9, acquires TX slot. On first call allocs 0xC-byte timer block and starts 1-second auto-reload timer (xTimerCreate, callback at DAT_00002e7c) for periodic re-send.

functions.name: can_diag_log_send
functions.note: Send diagnostic/event log entry over CAN. Vararg: param_1=opcode(u32), param_2=event-code(u16), param_3=extra-arg-count (0–3), then up to 3 word args on stack. Zeroes a 0x1E-byte packet, fills opcode+event-code, copies args, sends via FUN_000054e0 to obj+0x590 endpoint. No-ops if *DAT_00002ee0==0.

functions.name: gatt_attr_write_dispatch
functions.note: GATT-style attribute write dispatcher. Looks up attribute by 3-byte UUID (param_2[0..2]) via FUN_00004d3e. Dispatches by attr-type byte: type-0=direct write (calls handler piVar3[5]); type-1=read-only, returns 0; type-2=segmented reassembly (3 reassembly slots of 0x40 bytes each in obj+0x5AC, calls

functions.name: sensor_reg_block_write
functions.note: Writes a 0x200-byte register block to the sensor IC. Checks bus-lock (FUN_00000aec), performs register write (FUN_00000a28), checks mode (FUN_00000754) then calls either a direct function pointer or a vtable path to write the block. Logs diagnostic via can_diag_log_send on failure. Returns 0 on succ

functions.name: sensor_config_write
functions.note: Sensor peripheral configuration write with retry/fallback. Dispatches write to peripheral address 0xfa00 via vtable (FUN_00003204+0x10 vtable offset 0x10); on success reads back via FUN_00000790 at 0xf400, then calls sensor_reg_block_write for the 0xf400 block; logs diagnostic on failure. Returns 0,

functions.name: sensor_position_read
functions.note: Reads 12 bytes of position/angle register data from sensor at address 0xf400 into caller-supplied output buffer. Calls sensor_config_write; if result is -3 (unconfigured), zeroes the buffer via FUN_00005f62 (memset); on read success copies via FUN_00000790 into buf[0..11]. Returns 0 on success, -1 o

functions.name: sensor_speed_sample
functions.note: Reads a 6-byte position frame via sensor_position_read, validates it via memcmp (FUN_00005f72) against a reference pattern stored at 0x3378; if valid returns raw speed/count value from frame word [sp+0x1c]. Returns 0 on null handle or comparison mismatch; logs diagnostic on mismatch.

functions.name: motor_sensor_task_loop
functions.note: FreeRTOS task body for motor_sensor_task (infinite loop). Reads motor type byte and identifies variant (0x53=S, 0x41=A, 0x4c=L, 0x56=V). Calls sensor_speed_sample to get raw speed, scales via float (raw * cal_factor / 6.0) to compute normalized speed value stored to global. Every 30 ticks sends stat

functions.name: freertos_select_next_task
functions.note: VENDOR FreeRTOS port — vTaskSwitchContext / select-next-task. Checks scheduler-suspended flag; if suspended sets a pending-switch flag and returns. Otherwise clears pending flag, walks priority-ordered TCB ready list (0x14-byte stride array at DAT_00003754), finds highest-priority non-empty entry, r

functions.name: motor_sensor_hw_init_and_scheduler_start
functions.note: Top-level hardware initializer and FreeRTOS scheduler launcher. Configures RCC/GPIO (0x40000220), SPI/FLEXCOMM (0x400A0000), M_CAN/FDCAN (0x40006000) with bit-timing and filters, reads ADC (FUN_000004a0) and applies FPU scaling for current/voltage, writes WDT unlock sequence (0xAA/0x55), creates mot

functions.name: subscription_id_comparator
functions.note: Comparator for subscription list search: compares two subscription ID bytes (byte[1] then byte[0]) and returns the delta. Zero = match.

functions.name: subscription_list_search
functions.note: Linear search over flat subscription table (stride 0x2c). Calls comparator fn-ptr from param_1[2]. Returns matching index or 0xFFFFFFFF.

functions.name: subscription_find_entry
functions.note: Find subscription entry pointer by key. Returns (base + index*0x2c) or NULL if not found.

functions.name: subscription_find_entry_or_null
functions.note: Null-safe wrapper: returns NULL if handle is 0, else delegates to subscription_find_entry.

functions.name: can_tx_disable
functions.note: Disable CAN TX: clears MCAN mode-init bit (+0xe0), clears CCE (+0x54), sets INIT (+0xd4), waits for bus idle, clears app state fields +0x10 and +0x115.

functions.name: clock_prescaler_config
functions.note: Configure LPC546xx clock prescalers: writes divider values to 0x40000260 + (id*4) register file, encoding two prescaler fields packed into one uint32.

functions.name: gpio_debounce_clear
functions.note: Clear GPIO debounce latch: if bit 24 of +0x28 is set, re-writes +0x2c to clear the debounce flag.

functions.name: event_bit_set_if_unmasked
functions.note: Set event bit in param_1[9] only if not already set in *param_1 and the bit is in the mask param_1[9].

functions.name: gpio_pulse_wait
functions.note: GPIO pulse-and-wait: sets a GPIO pin via 0x40000120, polls 0x40000100 until set; then clears via 0x40000140, polls until clear. Used for SPI/flash CS sequencing.

functions.name: can_tx_submit_checked
functions.note: Validated CAN TX submit: returns error 1 if no handle, error 2 if no queue; else calls can_tx_submit(handle, queue, type+0x30, payload). Returns 0 on success.

functions.name: list_head_init
functions.note: FreeRTOS vListInitialise: initialises doubly-linked list sentinel node with circular self-pointers and zero count.

functions.name: list_insert_end
functions.note: FreeRTOS vListInsertEnd: inserts node before list sentinel (at end), sets owner, increments count.

functions.name: list_insert_ordered
functions.note: FreeRTOS vListInsert: sorted insertion by priority value; walks list until a node with higher value found, splices in before it.

functions.name: ringbuf_read_available
functions.note: Circular ring buffer query: returns bytes available to read = (write_head + size - read_head) % size.

functions.name: freertos_critical_section_enter
functions.note: FreeRTOS taskENTER_CRITICAL: saves BASEPRI, raises to 0x20 (mask priority), issues DSB+ISB. Returns saved BASEPRI in high word.

functions.name: ringbuf_write_free
functions.note: Circular ring buffer query: returns free space for writing = (size + write_head - 1 - read_head) % size. Halts on NULL handle.

functions.name: freertos_critical_section_exit
functions.note: FreeRTOS taskEXIT_CRITICAL: restores BASEPRI to saved value, issues DSB+ISB.

functions.name: freertos_pendsv_trigger
functions.note: Trigger PendSV context switch: writes 0x10000000 to ICSR (0xE000ED04), then DSB+ISB barriers.

functions.name: assert_nonnull_then_critical_exit
functions.note: Assert non-null under critical section: spins if param_1 is NULL, otherwise calls critical_section_exit + critical_section_leave.

functions.name: return_zero_stub
functions.note: Stub that unconditionally returns 0. Likely placeholder or dead callback slot.

functions.name: can_tx_queue_priority_select
functions.note: CAN TX queue arbitration: peeks both TX queues (priority 1 and 2), compares CAN IDs, selects the lower-priority ID queue for next transmission. Updates state at +0x24 and +0x20.

functions.name: list_remove_entry
functions.note: FreeRTOS uxListRemove: unlinks node from doubly-linked list, stitches prev/next, clears owner pointer, decrements count. Updates list head if removed node was head.

functions.name: freertos_block_free_if_nonnull
functions.note: Free memory block only if pointer is non-null; calls freertos_queue_free_block.

functions.name: freertos_tick_handler_wrapper
functions.note: SysTick body: enters critical section, calls freertos_tick_hook(), triggers PendSV if hook returns nonzero, exits critical section.

functions.name: freertos_queue_receive_from_isr
functions.note: FreeRTOS xQueueReceiveFromISR: dequeues one item from ring-style queue under critical section. Asserts handle non-null, handles reader-count tracking and ISR wakeup flag.

functions.name: freertos_event_group_set_bits
functions.note: FreeRTOS xEventGroupSetBits: iterates task wait list, ORs event bits, signals tasks whose wait masks are satisfied via freertos_queue_signal_waiting_task.

functions.name: can_frame_filter_dispatch
functions.note: CAN receive frame filter: unpacks 29-bit extended CAN ID from raw frame bytes, builds canonical form, calls registered filter callback (*param_3)(param_3, &canonical_id). Returns true if callback matches.

functions.name: can_tx_frame_write_chunks
functions.note: CAN TX frame write in ≤8-byte chunks via function pointer at param_1[0x10]. Handles CAN FD DLC wraparound (mode 1 = 4-bit nibble wrap, mode 2 = 3-bit nibble wrap).

functions.name: freertos_timer_advance_callback
functions.note: FreeRTOS timer internal: if timer +0x10 != 0, advances expiry time by increment (with size wrap), then calls FUN_00005f48 callback.

functions.name: freertos_timer_generic_command
functions.note: FreeRTOS xTimerGenericCommand: in task context calls freertos_timer_tick_process; in ISR context uses critical section + reader lock for deferred processing.

functions.name: ringbuf_read_consume
functions.note: Circular ring buffer consume: copies min(available, requested) bytes out of ring buffer with wraparound, advances read pointer.

functions.name: ringbuf_write_produce
functions.note: Circular ring buffer produce: copies data into ring buffer at write_head with wraparound, advances write pointer. Returns bytes written.

functions.name: subscription_list_add
functions.note: Add entry to subscription table: verifies count < capacity and no duplicate (via subscription_list_search returning -1), copies 0x2c-byte entry at [count], increments count. Returns 0 on success, 0xFFFFFFFF on failure.

functions.name: can_mailbox_buffer_setup
functions.note: M_CAN Tx/Rx buffer setup: reads TXBC/TXESC or RXBC/RXESC registers (+0xa0/0xa4 or +0xb0/0xb4), computes buffer element pointer, copies 8-byte CAN header. Updates put-index register.

functions.name: ringbuf_write_partial
functions.note: Ring buffer partial write with FD-mode flow control: checks FD bit (+0x1c), limits write length, calls ringbuf_write_produce. Returns actual bytes written or 0 if flow-controlled.

functions.name: freertos_stream_buffer_send
functions.note: FreeRTOS xStreamBufferSend (non-blocking path): writes data to stream buffer ring under critical section, signals waiting receiver via freertos_queue_recv_item, optionally triggers PendSV.

functions.name: can_frame_pack_and_enqueue
functions.note: Pack a raw CAN frame into a 16-byte queue item (DLC at [0], 29-bit ID at [4..7] from bytes 0..3 of raw frame, data at [8..15]) and enqueue to TX queue via queue_send_item.

functions.name: freertos_queue_send_from_isr_or_task
functions.note: FreeRTOS queue send dispatcher: if in task context calls queue_send_item directly; if in ISR context calls freertos_queue_receive_from_isr and triggers PendSV if a task was woken.

functions.name: freertos_stream_buffer_send_blocking
functions.note: FreeRTOS xStreamBufferSend (blocking path): validates args, writes partial data to ring buffer via ringbuf_write_partial, wakes or aborts receiver connection on full-buffer, handles both task and ISR contexts. Function body extends to 0x55f8.

functions.name: queue_receive_dispatch
functions.note: FreeRTOS queue receive helper; dispatches to registered callback (fn ptr at struct+0x14/+0x18) based on message byte (0x01=invoke callback, 0x02=call FUN_000020f4). Checks ISR vs task context.

functions.name: queue_send_from_isr_or_task
functions.note: FreeRTOS xQueueSend, ISR-aware. ISR path uses critical-section helpers (FUN_00004ea0/FUN_00004ed4). Task path calls portENTER_CRITICAL (FUN_00000920) / portEXIT_CRITICAL (FUN_0000093c). Triggers PendSV (write 0x10000000 to 0xE000ED04) when needed.

functions.name: flash_page_verify_and_advance
functions.note: Verifies a programmed flash page by reading back at offset (page+0x40)*0x200 and comparing. If more pages remain, advances page pointer and pre-fills next page buffer via memset(0xff, 0x200). Returns true if verified OK.

functions.name: flash_sector_data_write
functions.note: Writes caller-provided data to a flash sector identified by sector index (param_2). Looks up sector address via FUN_00004d3e, clears 4 bytes, then calls flash_write_sector (FUN_000024b8). Validates page range. Returns -1 on out-of-range.

functions.name: flash_sector_write_and_verify
functions.note: Programs a flash sector and verifies the write. Calls flash_page_verify_and_advance, reads calibration data via FUN_00000790(handle, 0x8134), calls flash_write_sector. Sets struct+0x15=1 (verified flag) if checksum matches. Writes error flag via memcpy.

functions.name: flash_erase_and_program_sector
functions.note: Erases up to 58 (0x3a) sectors starting at sector index param_3[0], count param_3[1], via FUN_00000aec (sector erase). Then programs a 512-byte page: fills header via memset+memcpy, calls flash_page_verify_and_advance. Returns 0 on success, -1 on error.

functions.name: can_frame_notify_subscribers
functions.note: Dispatches a CAN frame to registered subscriber callbacks. Builds 13-byte notification from frame object (CAN ID, length, data, type). For type 0 (data): calls fn ptr at param_1+0x5a4 with subscriber list at param_1+0x594. For type 1/2: calls FUN_000050cc (CAN TX queue send). Called by all can_send_

functions.name: can_frame_receive_and_dispatch
functions.note: Handles an incoming CAN frame: looks up frame descriptor via FUN_00004d3e, takes semaphore (FUN_00005154, timeout 0x7fffffff), invokes optional receive callback (fn ptr at struct+0x14), then calls can_frame_notify_subscribers. Returns without action if descriptor not found or semaphore fails.

functions.name: can_tx_submit_frame
functions.note: Submits a CAN TX frame. Constructs local CAN ID 0x8808, looks up TX descriptor via FUN_00004d3e, takes semaphore (timeout 100ms), copies frame data into descriptor buffer, calls can_frame_notify_subscribers. Called by can_tx_queue_flush and motor_sensor_hw_init_and_scheduler_start.

functions.name: can_tx_buf_write
functions.note: Appends data (param_3, length param_4) to the CAN TX ring buffer at current write position. Checks (write_pos + len) <= (page_count * page_size) before writing. Calls flash_write_sector to persist. Updates write pointer struct+8 on success. Returns 0 on success, -1 on overflow.

functions.name: can_bus_send_and_yield
functions.note: Flushes CAN TX buffer tail marker to flash if struct+0x15 flag is set (via flash_write_sector at end-of-buffer position), then triggers CAN controller TX (FUN_00000384) and signals CAN bus ready via FUN_00002970.

functions.name: can_bus_is_ready
functions.note: Tests CAN bus ready state. Gets CAN handle via FUN_00002928, queries ready state via FUN_00002970(handle, flag), releases handle. Returns 0 if ready, -1 if not ready or no handle.

functions.name: freertos_queue_create
functions.note: FreeRTOS xQueueGenericCreate: allocates heap_malloc(item_size*capacity+0x48), initialises QueueDefinition (head/tail/size fields, sentinel bytes 0xff at +0x44/+0x45), calls prvListInit (FUN_00004e32) for two embedded lists. Ends with portEXIT_CRITICAL (FUN_0000093c). Returns NULL if malloc fails.

functions.name: freertos_mutex_create
functions.note: FreeRTOS xSemaphoreCreateMutex: calls freertos_queue_create(1,0) then initialises mutex fields (count=0, head=0) and calls FUN_00001b10 (prvInitialiseMutex). Called only by motor_sensor_hw_init_and_scheduler_start.

functions.name: xPortSysTickHandler
functions.note: FreeRTOS SysTick ISR entry from vector table @0x3C. 2-instruction tail: mov r0,r4 / pop {r4,pc}. This is the epilogue of a larger SysTick body that precedes it in flash (the actual tick increment/scheduler call is in the code leading to 0x5b80).

functions.name: gatt_char_0x87_read_and_notify
functions.note: Reads 14 bytes of motor/sensor data via BLE handle (FUN_00002928/FUN_00004e10), looks up GATT attribute 0x87XX via FUN_00004d3e, copies data into attribute value buffer (14-byte loop), notifies subscribers via FUN_000054a8. Optionally sends response via memcmp+gatt_write_response_send. Called by ble

functions.name: gatt_char_0x91_read_and_notify
functions.note: Same pattern as gatt_char_0x87_read_and_notify but for attribute handle 0x91XX and a 16-byte payload. Called by ble_notify_char_0x91_and_update and motor_sensor_hw_init_and_scheduler_start.

functions.name: gatt_write_response_send
functions.note: Sends a GATT Write Response: heap_malloc(512), fills ATT PDU header (FUN_000007d4), copies attribute data via memcpy, submits via FUN_00000a04 + FUN_00004e10, verifies response via memcmp. Frees buffer. Returns true on error.

functions.name: ble_notify_char_0x87_and_update
functions.note: Builds a 14-byte BLE notification (1-byte type flag + 13-byte data from param_3), sends via gatt_write_response_send, calls gatt_char_0x87_read_and_notify to update the GATT attribute. Acquires/releases BLE connection handle. Returns -1 on error.

functions.name: ble_notify_char_0x91_and_update
functions.note: Same as ble_notify_char_0x87_and_update but 16-byte payload and calls gatt_char_0x91_read_and_notify. No callers found — likely invoked via fn pointer.

functions.name: flash_update_sector
functions.note: High-level flash config/calibration update. Reads 512-byte sector via FUN_00003204 (flash_read_sector), zeroes buffer, copies 12 new bytes at offset via memcpy, writes back with 64-second timeout via FUN_00003170 (flash_write_raw), re-reads and verifies. Returns 0 on success, -1 on write failure, -2

functions.name: assert_invalid_state_halt
functions.note: Assertion/panic halt. Checks version byte at *(struct+0x18-2) == 0x02; if not, enters infinite loop after FUN_00004ea0 (disable interrupts). Any other caller path also spins forever. No callers found — invoked via fn pointer or linker reference.

functions.name: memcmp
functions.note: Standard memcmp(s1, s2, n): byte-by-byte comparison, returns s1[i]-s2[i] at first mismatch or 0 if equal. Used throughout for CAN frame and GATT payload verification.

functions.name: memcpy
functions.note: Standard memcpy(dst, src, n): byte-by-byte forward copy. Used extensively for CAN frame data, GATT attribute values, and flash sector buffers.

functions.name: memset
functions.note: Standard memset(dst, val, n): fills n bytes with val. Ubiquitous for zeroing/filling flash buffers, CAN frame buffers, and GATT payload buffers.

functions.name: strncmp
functions.note: Standard strncmp(s1, s2, n): byte-by-byte compare stopping at n bytes, NUL, or mismatch. Returns s1[i]-s2[i] or 0. Called by FUN_00003310 (outside range).
